channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Australia charges ex‑soldier with 5 war‑crime murders in Afghanistan
item.description: Roberts-Smith is only the second Australian veteran of the Afghanistan campaign to be charged with a war crime.
item.content:encoded:
Roberts-Smith is only the second Australian veteran of the Afghanistan campaign to be charged with a war crime.

(Image credit: Anthony Devlin/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's deadline for an Iran deal looms. And, Artemis II crew begins the journey home
item.description: In a press conference last night, Trump reiterated threats against Iran if the country doesn't accept a deal by 8:00 p.m. ET tonight. And, the Artemis II crew are on their way back to Earth.
item.content:encoded:
In a press conference last night, Trump reiterated threats against Iran if the country doesn't accept a deal by 8:00 p.m. ET tonight. And, the Artemis II crew are on their way back to Earth.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: As Trump's deadline approaches, Iranian leaders respond in defiance
item.description: Hours away from President Trump's 8 pm ET Tuesday deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, attacks continued in the Persian Gulf with no agreement in sight. Trump has threatened to bomb Iranian bridges and power plants if a deal is not reached.
item.content:encoded:
Hours away from President Trump's 8 pm ET Tuesday deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, attacks continued in the Persian Gulf with no agreement in sight. Trump has threatened to bomb Iranian bridges and power plants if a deal is not reached.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: How Bill Phillips used flowing water to model the economy
item.description: Bill Phillips was an outsider to economics, but he used a machine and a chart to change the way we think about the government's role in a capitalist economy.
item.content:encoded:
Bill Phillips was an outsider to economics, but he used a machine and a chart to change the way we think about the government's role in a capitalist economy.

(Image credit: Julian Frost for Planet Money: A Guide To The Economic Forces That Shape Your Life
)
item.dc:creator: Alex Mayyasi

item.title: Beer cans, helium balloons and mortgages: An unexpected mix of things affected by war
item.description: It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.
item.content:encoded:
It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.

(Image credit: Brent Jones)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Taiwan's opposition leader arrives in China for a 'Journey of Peace'
item.description: The visit takes place ahead of President Trump's own summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping next month, where Taiwan is expected to be a top agenda item.
item.content:encoded:
The visit takes place ahead of President Trump's own summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping next month, where Taiwan is expected to be a top agenda item.

(Image credit: I-Hwa Cheng)
item.dc:creator: Ashish Valentine

item.title: Bracing for federal cuts, some states are already paring back Medicaid services
item.description: Paying for doulas to help birthing moms in maternity care deserts was a priority for Montana. But it halted the plan amid a budget shortfall and fears over coming federal Medicaid cuts.
item.content:encoded:
Paying for doulas to help birthing moms in maternity care deserts was a priority for Montana. But it halted the plan amid a budget shortfall and fears over coming federal Medicaid cuts.

(Image credit: Katheryn Houghton/KFF Health News)
item.dc:creator: Katheryn Houghton

item.title: Artemis II astronauts swung by the moon, broke an Apollo record, and saw an eclipse
item.description: The NASA moon mission completed several key milestones as its crew looped around the lunar body. It's expected to splash down on Earth on Friday.
item.content:encoded: The NASA moon mission completed several key milestones as its crew looped around the lunar body. It's expected to splash down on Earth on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: AI in the mental health care workforce is met with fear, pushback — and enthusiasm
item.description: Artificial intelligence tools that help mental health therapists take notes and keep records are quickly entering the marketplace. But some question the safety of AI in mental health care delivery.
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence tools that help mental health therapists take notes and keep records are quickly entering the marketplace. But some question the safety of AI in mental health care delivery.
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump repeats threat to bomb Iran's infrastructure if a deal isn't reached, strikes in the Middle East intensify as Trump's deadline looms, Artemis II crew heads home after historic moon mission.
item.content:encoded: Trump repeats threat to bomb Iran's infrastructure if a deal isn't reached, strikes in the Middle East intensify as Trump's deadline looms, Artemis II crew heads home after historic moon mission.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel
